element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must choose 2 of 6 companions (Bernard, Jeff, Michael, Razor, Syd, Wendy) to accompany Dave Miller; each has unique puzzle-solving abilities
15 action commands available (Walk To, Unlock, etc.) selected from menu in lower screen half
Intro shows Dave preparing to enter Edison mansion to rescue girlfriend Sandy Pantz
Sentient meteor crashed 20 years prior, brainwashed Edison family (Dr. Fred the mad scientist, Nurse Edna, Weird Ed); they kidnapped Sandy
All inhabitants except green tentacle are hostile; will throw players into dungeon or kill them if spotted
When character dies, player continues with remaining selected characters; game ends if all three die
Puzzles require specific character abilities; different solutions based on party composition
Cutscenes advance story and inform about offscreen events (Ron Gilbert coined the term)
Purple tentacle is hostile; green tentacle is friendly and non-hostile
Five possible endings based on character selection, survival, and accomplishments

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear exploration with tension between avoiding hostile NPCs and solving character-dependent puzzles; relief through discovery and progress; multiple valid solution paths allow player-paced tension management

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Different character abilities unlock different areas and solutions; party composition determines accessible puzzle paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory navigation and character ability familiarization; initial NPC avoidance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Complex multi-step puzzles requiring specific character combinations; increasing NPC encounter difficulty
difficulty_curve.late_game: Integration of multiple puzzle solutions; survival management of remaining party members
notable_spikes: Puzzles requiring rare character ability combinations

notable_level_patterns.description: Rooms and challenges that require specific character abilities (e.g., Syd/Razor musical instruments, Bernard appliance repair) to progress; forces player to consider party composition strategy
Areas with hostile inhabitants that will capture or kill players; stealth/evasion required rather than combat
Locations (implied by narrative) where green tentacle provides respite from hostile encounters
Single objective achievable through different paths depending on selected party members; creates replay value and logical branching
